title: A new methodology for inferring surface ozone from multispectral satellite measurements
justification: Here we propose a new method to infer spatial variability in surface ozone by combining multispectral ozone retrievals using radiances from the tropospheric emission spectrometer thermal infrared instrument and the ozone monitoring instrument ultratraviolet/visible instrument with a chemical reanalysis.

title: Global tropospheric ozone responses to reduced NOx emissions linked to the COVID-19 worldwide lockdowns
justification: The basic concentration and emission data used in the study can be downloaded freely from https://tes.jpl.nasa.gov/chemical-reanalysis/. K.M. and K.B. acknowledge the support of the NASA Atmospheric Composition: Aura Science Team Program (19-AURAST19-0044) and the TROPESS project.

title: The Carbon Cycle of Southeast Australia During 2019-2020: Drought, Fires, and Subsequent Recovery
justification: CrIS CO is provided by the NASA TRoposperhic Ozone and its Precursors from Earth System Sounding (TROPESS) and available from https://tes.jpl.nasa.gov.

title: Assessment of 16-year tropospheric ozone trends from the IASI Climate Data Record
justification: The IASI-CDR ozone product is evaluated against TROPESS (TRopospheric Ozone and its Precursors from Earth System Sounding) O 3 retrievals from the Cross-track Infrared Sounder (CrIS).

title: Identifying drivers of surface ozone bias in global chemical reanalysis with explainable machine learning
justification: The TROPESS chemical reanalysis product, TCR-2, as part of the MOMO-Chem framework was used in this study and is available on the NASA GED DISC website.

title: Single field-of-view sounder atmospheric product retrieval algorithm: establishing radiometric consistency for hyper-spectral sounder retrievals
justification: This paper gives an overview of the SiFSAP retrieval algorithm and assessment of SiFSAP atmospheric temperature, water vapor, clouds, and surface products derived from the Cross-track Infrared Sounder (CrIS) and Advanced Technology Microwave Sounder (ATMS) data.

title: Transport of substantial stratospheric ozone to the surface by a dying typhoon and shallow convection
justification: Satellite-based ozone vertical profiles measured by the AIRS and the OMI under the NASA TRopospheric Ozone and Precursors from Earth System Sounding (TROPESS) project were obtained from https://tes.jpl.nasa.gov/tropess/products/o3/

title: Crop water origins and hydroclimate vulnerability of global croplands
justification: The TES products are available at https://asdc.larc.nasa.gov/project/TES/TL2HDOLN_6 . The AIRS products are available via GES DISC at https://disc.gsfc.nasa.gov/datasets?keywords=tropess%20%2B%20airs%20%2B%20reanalysis&page=1 and EarthData at https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/data/catalog?keyword=TROPESS%20AIRS-Aqua%20Reanalysis

title: Intercomparison of global ground-level ozone datasets for health-relevant metrics
justification: We utilized surface ozone analysis fields from three chemical reanalysis products: the Tropospheric Chemistry Reanalysis Version 2 (TCR-2; Miyazaki et al., 2020b), the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service reanalysis (CAMS; Inness et al., 2019), and the GEOS-Chem reanalysis (GEOS; Qu et al., 2020b). Different from the machine learning and geostatistical ozone datasets, the chemical reanalysis products utilized satellite observations of atmospheric composition to produce three-dimensional profiles of atmospheric composition. The meteorological fields were nudged to the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) Interim Reanalysis meteorology. title: Evaluation of tropospheric ozone reanalyses with independent ozonesonde observations in East Asia
justification: The paper evaluates tropospheric ozone derived from seven reanalyses, including MERRA2 and TCR-2. MERRA2 is known to assimilate TES/TROPESS data, and TCR-2 is an updated Tropospheric Chemistry Reanalysis.

title: Technical note: Constraining the hydroxyl (OH) radical in the tropics with satellite observations of its drivers – first steps toward assessing the feasibility of a global observation strategy
justification: We use the total column water vapor standard physical retrieval as well as the seven water vapor layers listed in Table 1 (Susskind et al., 2014). Multiple studies have evaluated the accuracy of the AIRS H2O (v) column and layers retrievals in the remote tropical atmosphere

title: Validating tropospheric NO2 column density from GEMS version 3.0 and Tropospheric Chemistry Reanalysis version 2 from background to urban conditions over Japan: with a focus on diurnal variations
venue: Progress in Earth and Planetary Science
justification: The paper validates tropospheric NO2 VCD data from the Tropospheric Chemistry Reanalysis version 2 (TCR-2). TCR-2 is an updated reanalysis that ingests TROPESS/TES data.

title: Air pollution modulates trends and variability of the global methane budget
justification: The TCR-2 chemical reanalysis data were downloaded from https://tes.jpl.nasa.gov/tes/chemical-reanalysis/

title: Fingerprints of the COVID-19 economic downturn and recovery on ozone anomalies at high-elevation sites in North America and western Europe
justification: The paper cites Miyazaki et al. (2020), which is the TCR-2 chemical reanalysis. TCR-2 is explicitly mentioned as providing trends, indicating its use as a data product.

title: A simplified non-linear chemistry transport model for analyzing NO2 column observations: STILT–NOx
justification: we analyzed vertical distributions of NOx mixing ratios from TCR-2 (Miyazaki et al., 2020). TCR-2 is a global chemical reanalysis that includes full physical and chemical processes for various species and assimilates multiple satellite products of NO2, ozone, CO, and SO2.

title: Inferring and evaluating satellite-based constraints on NOx emissions estimates in air quality simulations
justification: The paper explicitly uses "NASA Tropospheric Chemical Reanalysis products 2 (TCR-2) satellite-inferred inventory" which is constrained using satellite observations and is a TROPESS-related product.

title: A satellite-data-driven framework to rapidly quantify air-basin-scale NOx emissions and its application to the Po Valley during the COVID-19 pandemic
justification: The NOx emissions from the JPL chemical reanalysis (Fig. 1 a) are constrained by assimilating O3, NO2, CO, HNO3, and SO2 from the OMI, GOME-2, SCIAMACHY, MLS, TES, and MOPITT satellite instruments (Miyazaki et al., 2020a)

title: Long-term trends and chemometric analysis of atmospheric air quality matrices in Nigeria (2003–2023) using NASA GIOVANNI satellite data
justification: using data from NASA satellites (Ozone Monitoring Instrument., OMI and Atmospheric Infrared Sounder, AIRS were investigated to provide insight into the spatiotemporal variations of surface temperatures, relative humidity and concentrations of pollutants such as nitrogen dioxide (NO₂), sulphur dioxide (SO₂), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO₂), methane (CH₄), formaldehyde (HCHO), and ozone (O₃). Data was collected and analyzed using the NASA GIOVANNI platform

title: NO₂ Emission Estimation in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam Using Modeling and OMI Satellite Data
venue: Bulletin of Environmental Contamination and Toxicology
justification: The LMAM model demonstrated a strong correlation with TROPESS Chemical Reanalysis (TCR) NOx data (Spearman’s r = 0.71 in 2019; r = 0.70 in 2020), confirming its reliability for trend analysis.

title: Converging evidence for reduced global atmospheric oxidation in 2020
justification: This contrast is further supported by opposite anomalies in hemispheric tropospheric ozone observed by satellite.

title: Spatiotemporal variations and relationships of absorbing aerosol-radiation-gross primary productivity over China
justification: The AIRS data were obtained from https://disc.gsfc.nasa.gov/datasets/ summary accessed on 1 July 2020.

title: Strong Wildfires in the Russian Federation in 2021 Detected Using Satellite Data
justification: The abstract explicitly cites and links to the "AIRS/AMSU/HSB version 6 level 3 product user guide", indicating the use of AIRS data, which is part of TROPESS.

title: Global decadal measurements of methanol, ethene, ethyne, and HCN from the Cross-track Infrared Sounder
justification: TROPESS CrIS CO data are available at https://doi.org/10.5067/LS66YMQDSPRI (Bowman, 2021).
